text: Hikit has attempted to disable driver signing verification by tampering with several Registry keys prior to the loading of a rootkit driver component.
Malware modified the /proc/ directory entry for its malicious process to appear as a legitimate system service.

APT32 has used hidden or non-printing characters to help masquerade service names, such as appending a Unicode no-break space character to a legitimate service name. APT32 has also impersonated the legitimate Flash installer file name "install_flashplayer.exe".
Dragonfly has used SMB for C2.
SMOKEDHAM has used Google Drive and Dropbox to host files downloaded by victims via malicious links.
The phishing email directed recipients to click a malicious link that redirected to a credential harvesting page.
An attacker used wscript.exe to execute a script while evading PowerShell execution restrictions.
The attacker deleted the compromised cloud instance that was used for credential harvesting to eliminate evidence.
Line Runner is a persistent Lua-based web shell.
Threat Group-3390 has obtained stolen valid certificates, including from VMProtect and the Chinese instant messaging application Youdu, for their operations.
Ebury has implemented a fallback mechanism to begin using a DGA when the attacker hasn't connected to the infected system for three days.
Sliver can leverage multiple techniques to bypass User Account Control (UAC) on Windows systems.
Bonadan can XOR-encrypt C2 communications.
Agent Tesla has exploited Office vulnerabilities such as CVE-2017-11882 and CVE-2017-8570 for execution during delivery.
Phishing messages containing links to credential-harvesting forms were distributed via social media.
SocGholish has been named `AutoUpdater.js` to mimic legitimate update files.
MirageFox is likely loaded via DLL hijacking into a legitimate McAfee binary.
An attacker executed 'reg add HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run /v UpdateService /t REG_SZ /d "C:\malware.exe" /f' to establish persistence.
The malware queried Facebook profiles of executives to gather information about office locations.
Using bash, the malware ran 'cat /etc/passwd' to extract valid user account information from the system.
Malicious code exploited stolen SSH private keys to authenticate to other systems in the network.
The attacker updated the profile.json configuration to add fabricated social connections and interests.
Malware deployed a malicious Lambda function that executed code to exfiltrate data.
APT28 has saved files with hidden file attributes.

An adversary loaded file.dll using odbcconf.exe to circumvent application controls.
Neoichor can check for Internet connectivity by contacting bing.com with the request format `bing.com?id=`.
Comnie appends a total of 64MB of garbage data to a file to deter any security products in place that may be scanning files on disk.
The cybercriminal leveraged a breached third-party administrator account to gain unauthorized administrative control over the target Office 365 environment.
Malware used 'msbuild.exe' to compile and execute a malicious payload while evading detection.
Lumma Stealer has leveraged legitimate applications to then side-load malicious DLLs during execution.

Maze has disabled dynamic analysis and other security tools including IDA debugger, x32dbg, and OllyDbg. It has also disabled Windows Defender's Real-Time Monitoring feature and attempted to disable endpoint protection services.
The attacker executes profiles command to install a malicious configuration profile.
An attacker utilized the Azure Serial Console feature to bypass network security controls and access a virtual machine.
Mis-Type has temporarily stored collected information to the files `“%AppData%\{Unique Identifier}\HOSTRURKLSR”` and `“%AppData%\{Unique Identifier}\NEWERSSEMP”`.
A compromised account was used to query the domain for all service accounts vulnerable to Kerberoasting.
ROADTools can enumerate Azure AD groups.

Emotet has been observed injecting in to Explorer.exe and other processes.
The Autorun.inf file was altered to launch a hidden payload when the removable device was connected to any system.
BabyShark has executed the ipconfig /all command.

During CostaRicto, the threat actors obtained open source tools to use in their operations.
Grandoreiro has named malicious browser extensions and update files to appear legitimate.
The threat actor extracted a security token using DuplicateToken to gain elevated access rights.
The threat actor's payload employed NtQueryInformationProcess() to query for debugger presence and alter its behavior accordingly.
The script searched through online domain tools to identify IP ranges and hosting information for the victim organization.
